nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.id: loc-约翰-弗里德里希-格梅林-8a33c9ca
nodes.name: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林
nodes.summary: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联16种鸟类，包括灰穴䳍、杂色穴䳍、葡萄胸鴨等16种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸭科
nodes.summary: 鸭科（Anatidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联162种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、白背鸭、细斑树鸭等162种鸟类。

nodes.name: 雁形目
nodes.summary: 雁形目（Anseriformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联166种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、角叫鴨、黑颈冠叫鸭等166种鸟类。

nodes.name: 北美洲
nodes.summary: 北美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括紅嘴樹鴨、黑嘴天鹅、加拿大雁等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 美洲绿翅鸭
nodes.summary: 美洲绿翅鸭（Green-winged teal，学名 Anas carolinensis），分类上属于雁形目，并归入鸭科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林、北美洲、阿留申群島，常见栖息地包括湿地、沼泽。
locations: 约翰·弗里德里希·格梅林

nodes.name: 阿留申群島
nodes.summary: 阿留申群島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括帝雁、美洲绿翅鸭、短尾鹱等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The green-winged teal (Anas carolinensis) or American teal is a common and widespread duck that breeds in the northern areas of North America except on the Aleutian Islands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The green-winged teal (Anas carolinensis) or American teal is a common and widespread duck that breeds in the northern areas of North America except on the Aleutian Islands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The green-winged teal (Anas carolinensis) or American teal is a common and widespread duck that breeds in the northern areas of North America except on the Aleutian Islands

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: They also inhabit arctic tundra and semidesert communities. Within the above associations, green-winged teal commonly inhabit wetland communities dominated by bulrushes (Scirpus spp.), cattails (Typha spp.), sedges (Carex spp.), pondweeds (Potamogeton spp.) and other emergent and aquatic vegetation. Green-winged teal frequently nest in grasses, sedge meadows, or on dry hillsides having brush or aspen (Populus spp.) cover. Near Brooks, Alberta, green-winged teal nests were found most often in beds of rushes (Juncus spp.), and in western Montana most nests were located under greasewood (Sarcobatus spp.). Green-winged teal inhabit inland lakes, marshes, ponds, pools, and shallow streams with dense emergent and aquatic vegetation.Johnsgard, Paul A. (1979)
